I want to highly appreciate my data mentor, Tony Cheung, for his constant guidance on my data journey.

 

Do you want to keep learning about SAP Datasphere?

SAP continues to add innovations into Datasphere and you can check them publicly in the roadmap.
SAP also continues to grow partnerships with category leaders such as Collibra, Databricks, Google Cloud, among others, to deliver even more effective, efficient and holistic enterprise data and analytics strategies for a wide range of organizations. 

Also, take a look at these learning journeys on the SAP Learning Portal.

entry.title: Steps to Create a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere for an Existing CDS View in S/4HANA
entry.content:
This guide walks you through the process of setting up a replication flow in SAP Data sphere for a CDS view that exists in your S/4HANA on-premise system.

Why Use SAP Data sphere for CDS View Replication from S/4HANA?

In a landscape where data integration across systems is crucial, SAP Datasphere offers a powerful solution for extracting, transforming, and loading data from SAP S/4HANA into a centralized data repository. By replicating CDS (Core Data Service) views from S/4HANA to SAP Datasphere, you create an efficient data pipeline that enables seamless data integration, analysis, and consumption across various platforms.

Now let's discuss the steps : 

Step 1: Set Up Cloud Connector for SAP Datasphere Subaccount

  1. Login to your Cloud Connector account
  2. Select Your Sub account 

Ensure the sub account chosen matches the one associated with your SAP Datasphere account. Select the sap dataSphere sub account from the list ( need admin rights in datasphere sub account for your account to be visible for choosing the sub-account).

  1. Create a New Connection

        Navigate to Cloud to On-Premise and create a new connection.

Choose backend-type = ABAP and Use the RFC protocol for this connection. 

4. Add Required Functions to Your Connection

Below are the details what are specific functionality of each-:

  • DDIF_ Prefix -: Functions related to the SAP Data Dictionary Interface, typically used for reading table or structure definitions from the on-premise system.
  • DHAMB_ Prefix -: Likely refers to Application Management services, including background processes and job handling.
  • DHAPE_ Prefix -: Associated with handling Application Protocols or Process Engines, often used for process management and monitoring.
  • RFC Prefix -: Used for standard SAP Remote Function Calls, enabling communication between the cloud and the on-premise system.

These prefixes represent different categories of RFC destinations, facilitating various operations between cloud applications and on-premises SAP systems through the SAP Cloud Connector.

Step 2: Create a Connection in SAP Data sphere

  1. Login to SAP Data sphere Navigate to your SAP Data sphere account and go to the **Connections** section.

 

2. Select the Space for the Connection                                                                                                          Choose the space in which you want to create this connection.

 

3. Create a New Connection                                                                                                                            Choose **SAP S/4HANA On-Premise** as the connection type. Click on the (+) sign to create new connection       

Provide the Application Server, System Number, and Client of your on-premise system.You can get these information from your GUI(SAP - logon) Screen for the respective connection.

   Set Use Cloud Connector to True

Enter the Virtual Host and Port, which you can retrieve from the connection built in Cloud Connector.

4. Provide Credentials                                                                                                                                       Enter the **Username** and **Password** used for logging into the on-premises system (e.g., if you have   made the connection for QOI 310 then use your credential which you use in logon to login).

5. Validate the Connection                                                                                                                       Once all information is entered, validate your connection to ensure everything is set up correctly.

 

Step 3: Modify Your CDS View for Data Extraction

To enable data extraction in SAP Datasphere, add the following annotation to your CDS view: 

 

 

 

 

: {
    dataExtraction: {
        enabled: true,
        delta.changeDataCapture: {
            automatic: true
        }
    }
}

 

 

For this blog i have used JEST table as an example for replication.

Step 4: Create a Replication Flow in SAP Data sphere

  1. Login to SAP Data sphere                                                                                                                        Select the space where you want to create the replication flow.
  2. Navigate to Data Builder                                                                                                                        Click on Data Builder, then click Create New Replication Flow.                                                                   
     

     

  3. Select the Source -:  Choose the **Source Connection** as the one you've just created.

Select the CDS view as the Source Container.

 
 

 

  1. Select the Destination -: For the Target Connection, you can either, let SAP Data sphere create a local table where the data from the on-premises system will be replicated, or Create a local table in SAP Data sphere and choose it as the Target Container.

in this case The target connection is SAP Data sphere and Z_T_DCS_PER1 is the local table which will be automatically created by data sphere itself when deployed.

5. Save the Replication Flow -: Give the replication flow a suitable name and save it .              

6. Deploy and Run the Replication Flow

After deploying start the RUN.
7. Monitor Replication Flow -: Click on the Open in Flow Monitor in the Run Status tab.

 


 

Here you can check the number of records it fetched and if the run is complete success or not

A local table is created with the mapped fields you can visit from the data builder screen.

entry.title: Perform Percentage Calculations in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Objective

To perform and capture different percentage metrics in SAP Datasphere to calculate different types of percentages as per the business use cases and display various percentage values with the symbol ‘%’ as a suffix. As it is known in BEx query designer there are pre-built percentage functions that are used to calculate different types of percentages as per the business requirement of the report. These functions are used in calculated key figures/formulas of a query such as below -

  1. %GT - Percentage share of Grand total (Overall Result)
  2. %CT - Percentage share of Cumulative Total (Values in a Result)

This article is completely based on my learning experience for the above two such similar functions in SAP Datasphere. I hope, this illustration and technical approach will be useful for those who are looking to perform percentage calculations based on the reporting and analytics use cases.

Approach

  1. Option to calculate using a Data Flow script node using Python and persist in a table
  2. Option to calculate using Windows functions in a SQL based view
  3. Option to calculate using Graphical View  

Illustration

  1. Option to calculate using a Data Flow script node using Python and persist in a table

 

%GT - Percentage Total of Grand total

Specifies how high the percentage share of the grand total. Grand total here means the result of the aggregation at the highest level of the list.

Code Snippet using Python:

def transform(df):

    df['ORDERQUANTITY'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].astype('float64')

    df['TOTAL'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].sum()

    df['PERCENT'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'] / df['TOTAL']

    return df

%CT – Percentage share of a values of a result

Specifies how high the percentage amount is regarding the result. Result here means the result of the aggregation at the next-highest level (intermediate result).

 

Code Snippet using Python

def transform(df):

    df['ORDERQUANTITY'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'].astype('float64')

    df['TOTAL'] = df.groupby('SALESDOCUMENT')['ORDERQUANTITY'].transform('sum')

    df['PERCENT'] = df['ORDERQUANTITY'] / df['TOTAL']

    return df

Observations:

  1. Both the calculations in same node but different data frames for consolidation. Just for illustration purposes, the above code snippet is split into two data frames.
  2. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  3. This option could be considered in the scenarios of fixed reporting and absence of dynamics with the dimensions2. Option to calculate using Windows functions in a SQL based view

Sample Dataset

Code Snippet using SQL view creation for calculating percentages:

select SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, sum(ORDERQUANTITY) as ORDERQUANTITY,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over ()  as GRAND_TOT_AMT,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (ORDER BY SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM)  as CUM_TOT_AMT,

sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (PARTITION BY SALESDOCUMENT)  as ORDR_TOT_AMT,

CAST ( ( SUM(ORDERQUANTITY) / sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over () ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) as GRAND_TOT_PERCENT,

 CAST ( ( SUM(ORDERQUANTITY) / sum("ORDERQUANTITY") over (PARTITION BY SALESDOCUMENT) ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) as CUM_TOT_PERCENT

  from( 

       select SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, ORDERQUANTITY from "TST_VW_PERCENTAGE_CHCK" ----Change the view or table name and accordingly the fields

        )

        group by SALESDOCUMENT, SALESDOCUMENTITEM, ORDERQUANTITY

Observations:

  1. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  2. This option could be considered in the scenarios of reporting primarily inclusive of dynamic filtering with the dimensions without any persistence3. Option to calculate totals and percentages using Graphical views

Code Snippet

  • Design the Graphical view calling thrice with inner join…while first node brings in at Sales Document Level which is the first, second node calculates the Grand total while there is no dimension that should be part of the result set, third node calculates the cumulative total based of the first level highest for example here is the sales document level.

 

  • Perform cumulative and Grand percentages at the final node level in a calculation node to be able to consume for analytics and insights.

 For example -

On the preview the result set to be

Observations:

  1. Alternatively calculate the percentages in the following layers or the Analytical Model
  2. This option could be considered too in scenarios of filtering with dimensions but of course the view or the table needs to be called thrice in the form of a graphical design.

To display percentage '%' for those metrics:

Refer to the following community blog posted- Display percentage for a calculated measure in Da... - SAP Community
